title: Market Brief — Sunday, May 10, 2026
summary: Compute is tightening fast. GB200 availability sits at a severe-shortage 10%, B200 is scarce at 25%, and H200 is constrained at 45%, while H100 utilization surged 14.8pp to 97.7% in 30 days. A100s show oversupply at 85% available with utilization sliding 6.2pp to 71.3%, signaling legacy demand is migrating to newer silicon. H200 OCPI volatility at 88.7% annualized screams unstable pricing on thin liquidity.
paragraphs: Compute is tightening fast. GB200 availability sits at a severe-shortage 10%, B200 is scarce at 25%, and H200 is constrained at 45%, while H100 utilization surged 14.8pp to 97.7% in 30 days. A100s show oversupply at 85% available with utilization sliding 6.2pp to 71.3%, signaling legacy demand is migrating to newer silicon. H200 OCPI volatility at 88.7% annualized screams unstable pricing on thin liquidity.

Margins are brutal on older GPUs. Running Llama 70B on A100 costs $9.48/M tokens TCO-adjusted (1.25x of $7.58), meaning Medium-tier API sellers at $3/M are losing roughly $6.48 per million tokens. B200 is the only hardware where mid-tier inference breaks even. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.378/M output is burning cash against any self-hosted baseline. OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 compute is undervalued: spot median $4.20/hr against OCPI of $4.09/hr with 83.7% utilization climbing 21.6pp monthly, buy forward capacity now before availability drops below scarcity threshold. A100 is overvalued for inference workloads at $0.90/hr spot given declining utilization and negative margins at every tier below Premium, rotate out.
bullets: 25% B200 availability with 21.6pp utilization gain in 30 days signals scarcity is weeks away.
10% GB200 availability marks a severe shortage with no transparent spot pricing yet established.
97.7% H100 utilization is effectively sold out despite 70% nominal availability, reflecting reservation lock-in.
$0.378/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 sits roughly $4.50 below TCO-adjusted cost on H200 hardware.
21.41 LLMflation index means API prices have fallen 79% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$88.7% annualized H200 OCPI volatility is nearly double A100 volatility at 46.6%, flagging pricing risk.
